title: Data Validation
summary: Data Validation restricts what users can type into a cell, enabling dropdown lists, number ranges, and date limits to keep data clean and consistent.
syntax: Select cells > Data > Data Validation > choose Allow type (List, Whole number, Date) > set criteria
jobUseCases: Add a Department dropdown so staff pick from approved values instead of free typing
Restrict a Quantity column to whole numbers between 1 and 1000 on an order form
Force a Joining Date to fall on or after the company start date in HR records
Limit a text-comment field to 50 characters to fit a downstream system
Prevent duplicate invoice numbers from being entered using a formula rule

examples.title: Dropdown list from typed values
examples.formula: Data > Data Validation > Allow: List > Source: North,South,East,West
examples.explanation: Select the Region cells and choose List, then type the options separated by commas. A dropdown arrow appears so users pick North, South, East, or West, preventing typos like 'Norht' that would break PivotTables.

examples.title: Dropdown from a range
examples.formula: Data > Data Validation > Allow: List > Source: =$H$2:$H$10
examples.explanation: Point the Source at a range (or a named range / Table column) holding your master list of departments. The dropdown updates automatically as you maintain that list, which is cleaner than hard-coding values.

examples.title: Restrict to a number range
examples.formula: Data > Data Validation > Allow: Whole number > between 1 and 1000
examples.explanation: Choose Whole number and set minimum 1, maximum 1000. Anyone entering 0, 5000, or text gets an error message, ensuring an order Quantity stays within valid limits.

examples.title: Custom formula to block duplicates
examples.explanation: Select the Invoice No range, choose Custom, and enter =COUNTIF($A$2:$A$100,A2)=1. The cell is only accepted when that value appears exactly once in the column, so duplicate invoice numbers are rejected on entry.

commonMistakes: Expecting validation to clean existing data; it only blocks new entries, so paste-overs and prior bad values stay (use Data > Circle Invalid Data to find them)
Pasting into a validated cell, which can bypass or overwrite the rule entirely
Forgetting to lock references ($H$2:$H$10) so the source range shifts when copied down
Leaving 'Ignore blank' checked and wondering why empty cells are allowed
Not setting a clear Error Alert message, leaving users confused about why their entry was rejected
practiceTips: Base list dropdowns on a Table column so new options appear automatically without editing the rule.
Use the Input Message tab to show a helpful tooltip when the cell is selected.
Use Data > Circle Invalid Data to audit values that were entered or pasted before the rule existed.
For dependent dropdowns, combine Data Validation with the INDIRECT function on named ranges.
